title: Collaboration Between Sealaska and Alaska Longline Fishermen’s Association  Fills Freezers for Subsistence-Based Sealaska Shareholders
summary: (September 25, 2020) – Shareholders from around Southeast Alaska filled their smokehouses, pantries and freezers with the rich red of sockeye salmon this week, courtesy of a unique partnership between Sealaska and the Alaska Longline Fishermen’s Association (ALFA), in conjunction with tribal leaders...

title: Building Leaders and Supporting Community Through TRAYLS Program
summary: This summer, the TRAYLS (Training Rural Alaskan Youth Leaders and Students) crew in Kake continued a solemn but purposeful task — improving trails on Grave Island. The work started in 2019, when the death of a local resident who was to be interred on the island prompted a request of the TRAYLS crew....

title: American Indian Science and Engineering Society National Conference Goes Virtual!
summary: For the second year in a row, Sealaska is a Native Circle sponsor for the American Indian Science and Engineering Society (AISES) national conference. title: AST Sub-Legal Moose: Soldtona - Irish Hills [AK20065938]
summary: On 9/18/2020 Todd Moore, age 67 of Soldotna reported that he shot a sub-legal bull moose in GMU 15B. AWT confirmed that the bull was sub-legal and issued Moore a citation with a mandatory court appearance in the Kenai District Court. The antlers were seized and the meat donated to a charity.

title: Southeast Families Receive 49,000 Pounds of Salmon to Help with Difficult Season
summary: This month, a partnership between Sealaska and the Alaska Longline Fisheries Association (ALFA) will result in the distribution of 49,000 pounds of salmon to families reliant on subsistence fishing from Yakutat to Hydaburg. ALFA received a $250,000 grant from the Alaska Community Foundation and Catc...

title: Seafood at Sealaska
summary: In the next 50 years, the world’s population will grow by nearly 3 billion to a total of 10.5 billion people, according to the United Nations. title: Sealaska Support Brings Joy, Growth and Connection Through Yakutat Surf Camp
summary: Late this summer, a series of happy coincidences led 40 Yakutat kids out of the doldrums of the pandemic and into the icy waters of the North Pacific for a series of surf camps in Yakutat’s legendary waves.
